📋1. Introduction

Customer churn — the rate at which customers discontinue service — is one of the most critical metrics in the telecom industry. With acquisition costs 5–7× higher than retention costs, predicting churn early and intervening proactively can save millions in annual revenue.

This project builds an end-to-end churn prediction system combining SQL-based data exploration, machine learning classification, model explainability (SHAP), and customer segmentation to deliver actionable retention strategies.

📝2. Abstract

We analyzed 5,000 telecom customers across 13 features including demographics, contract details, service add-ons, and behavioral signals (call duration, complaints, recharge frequency). Using SQL aggregation for initial insights and Python-based ML for prediction, we trained Logistic Regression and Random Forest models with SMOTE-balanced classes. The Random Forest model consistently outperformed, achieving the highest AUC-ROC score. SHAP explainability revealed that contract type, tenure, and complaints were the strongest churn drivers. Customers were segmented into At Risk, Dormant, and Loyal groups with tailored retention strategies.

🛠️3. Tools Used

🐍 Python 3.x
🗃️ SQLite + SQL
📊 pandas / numpy
🤖 scikit-learn
⚖️ imbalanced-learn
📈 matplotlib / seaborn
🔍 SHAP
📓 Jupyter-style scripts
📄 HTML/CSS Reporting

📌4. Steps Involved

# Step Description
1Data GenerationCreated synthetic dataset of 5,000 customers with realistic churn patterns
2SQL Aggregation10 analytical queries: churn rate by contract, complaints, tenure, revenue loss
3Data CleaningHandled ~3% missing values (TotalCharges, CallDuration), type conversion
4Feature EncodingLabel-encoded 6 categorical variables for ML compatibility
5Feature ScalingStandardScaler on training set only — prevents data leakage
6EDA6 visualizations: churn distribution, tenure/charges analysis, correlation heatmap
7SMOTEBalanced minority class (churned) to improve recall on churn detection
8Model TrainingLogistic Regression + Random Forest with 80/20 stratified split
9EvaluationCompared Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1, AUC-ROC + confusion matrices
10ExplainabilitySHAP TreeExplainer for feature impact interpretation
11SegmentationClassified customers into At Risk / Dormant / Loyal using churn probability
12ReportingGenerated this self-contained HTML report with embedded analytics

📊5. Exploratory Data Analysis

EDA reveals the key patterns in churn behavior before modeling.

🤖6. Model Performance

Both models were trained on SMOTE-balanced data and evaluated on the original test set.

Model Accuracy Precision Recall F1 Score AUC-ROC
Logistic Regression 0.6450 0.5320 0.6084 0.5676 0.6910
Random Forest 0.6660 0.5736 0.4987 0.5335 0.6957

🔍7. Feature Importance & Explainability

Understanding why the model predicts churn is essential for building trust and informing strategy.

👥8. Customer Segmentation

Customers are segmented based on their predicted churn probability for targeted retention.

Segment Count Avg Churn Prob Avg Monthly Charge Avg Tenure Actual Churn Rate
● At Risk 1,870 68.7% $71.77 16.4 mo 88.9%
● Dormant 1,992 32.7% $69.73 20.8 mo 12.0%
● Loyal 1,138 12.9% $66.61 35.9 mo 1.1%

💡9. Business Recommendations

📋 Contract Conversion Campaign

Month-to-Month contracts have the highest churn rate. Offer 15-20% discounts to migrate these customers to annual contracts — each conversion reduces churn risk by up to 20%.

📞 24-Hour Complaint Resolution SLA

Every additional complaint increases churn probability significantly. Implement a fast-track resolution lane for customers with 2+ complaints and close the loop with a satisfaction callback.

🎯 First-Year Engagement Program

New customers (tenure < 12 months) churn at the highest rate. Deploy welcome calls at Day 7, 30, and 90 with onboarding support and early-bird loyalty rewards.

🔄 Auto-Recharge Incentives

Low recharge frequency signals disengagement. Offer ₹50/month discounts for setting up auto-recharge — this locks in customers and reduces payment friction.

🛡️ Value-Add Bundling

Customers without OnlineSecurity or TechSupport churn more. Bundle these services at a 30% discount for at-risk segments to increase perceived value and switching cost.

📊 Deploy ML Scoring in CRM

Run weekly batch scoring with the Random Forest model. Flag customers with >50% churn probability for proactive outreach by the retention team. Expected to save 15-25% of at-risk revenue.

✅10. Conclusion

This end-to-end churn analysis demonstrates that customer churn in the telecom industry is predictable and preventable. By combining SQL-driven data exploration with machine learning, we identified that contract type, tenure, complaints, and recharge behavior are the primary churn drivers.

The Random Forest model provides high prediction accuracy, while SHAP analysis ensures model transparency for business stakeholders. The three-tier customer segmentation (At Risk / Dormant / Loyal) enables differentiated retention strategies with measurable ROI.

Key Takeaway: Investing in proactive retention (contract conversion, complaint resolution, first-year engagement) is 5–7× more cost-effective than acquiring new customers. This analysis provides the data-driven foundation for such programs.
